commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=166fc81f96ea6c1d5cffcf75e55368f2cc152937)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a `DEFAULT_SANDBOX_PIDS_LIMIT = 1024` constant and uses it as the fallback `pidsLimit` when sandbox Docker config omits both agent and global values.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: resolve Docker sandbox config with no global or per-agent `pidsLimit`, then pass it to `buildSandboxCreateArgs`; current main leaves the value undefined and emits no `--pids-limit`. I did not create a real Docker container in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer security/product approval of the default and opt-out semantics plus contributor-provided real Docker proof; this is not a safe automated repair while proof and policy are unresolved.

target.title: fix(security): add default pidsLimit for sandbox containers
target.body:
## Summary

Sandbox containers were created without a default pidsLimit, making them vulnerable to fork bomb attacks that could exhaust host resources.

## Security Impact

- **Prevents fork bomb attacks**: Malicious or compromised agents cannot execute `:(){ :|:& };:` style attacks
- **Limits container processes**: Containers can create at most 1024 processes
- **Protects host system**: Prevents PID/memory exhaustion

## Changes

1. Added `DEFAULT_SANDBOX_PIDS_LIMIT = 1024` constant in `src/agents/sandbox/constants.ts`
2. Applied default value in `src/agents/sandbox/config.ts` when no explicit limit is configured

## Before

```typescript
pidsLimit: agentDocker?.pidsLimit ?? globalDocker?.pidsLimit,  // NO DEFAULT!
```

## After

```typescript
pidsLimit: agentDocker?.pidsLimit ?? globalDocker?.pidsLimit ?? DEFAULT_SANDBOX_PIDS_LIMIT,
```

Fixes #38604
